item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence tells Meet the Press Democrats have “lost their mind” and Republicans can still hold onto their Senate majority, but says the party has “lost our way” after Texas Republicans nominated Ken Paxton for Senate.

media:content.media:title: Mike Pence says GOP ‘lost our way’ after nominating Ken Paxton ‘but Democrats have lost their mind’
media:content.media:description: Former Vice President Mike Pence tells Meet the Press Democrats have “lost their mind” and Republicans can still hold onto their Senate majority, but says the party has “lost our way” after Texas Republicans nominated Ken Paxton for Senate.

item.title: Mike Pence says GOP ‘lost our way’ after nominating Ken Paxton ‘but Democrats have lost their mind’

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday suggested canceling planned musical performances at the Great American State Fair in Washington this summer after several artists pulled out of the event.“We should have a giant MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN RALLY, for 250, instead of having overpriced singers, who nobody wants to hear, whose music is boring, and yet who do nothing but complain,” Trump said in a post on Truth Social Saturday afternoon.“Cancel it,” Trump said, before launching into a separate

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump speaking while half-obscured by a white column outside the White House.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump outside the White House on May 25, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Young MC, left, and Morris Day both declined to participate after saying they learned the event was political in nature.
media:content.media:description: Young MC, left, and Morris Day both declined to participate after saying they learned the event was political in nature.

item.title: Trump suggests canceling performances at ‘Great American State Fair’ after artists back out

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and captured a strategic mountain in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in 26 years.

media:content.media:text: An Israeli flag flies over the medieval Beaufort Castle, known locally as Qalaat al-Shaqif or Shaqif Arnoun, as seen from the Marjayoun area of southern Lebanon on May 31.
media:content.media:description: An Israeli flag flies over the medieval Beaufort Castle, known locally as Qalaat al-Shaqif or Shaqif Arnoun, as seen from the Marjayoun area of southern Lebanon on May 31.

item.title: Netanyahu vows to expand Israel’s grip on Lebanon after deepest incursion in 26 years

item.dc:creator: Lauren Wilson
item.description: A24’s “Backrooms” — from 20-year-old Kane Parsons — broke box office records this weekend, marking the latest example of an indie horror hit helmed by a YouTuber.

media:content.media:text: A three way split composite image of Kane Parsons, left, Curry Barker, and Markiplier.
media:content.media:description: From left, Kane Parsons, Curry Barker, Markiplier.

media:content.media:text: Renate Reinsve stands in front of a wall with a rectangle drawn in blue lines, and her shadow is cast next to her on the wall
media:content.media:description: From left, Kane Parsons, Curry Barker, Markiplier.

item.title: Meet Hollywood’s new masters of horror: YouTubers

item.dc:creator: Tyler Kingkade
item.description: “The Digital Delusion” by Jared Cooney Horvath connects the collapse in standardized test scores to the rise of education technology in public schools.

media:content.media:text: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.
media:content.media:description: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.

media:content.media:text: Jared Cooney Horvath predicted ed tech would be the next big debate after schools banned cellphones.
media:content.media:description: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.

media:content.media:text: Parents brought copies of "The Digital Delusion" to give to school board members in Thousand Oaks, Calif., on Feb. 4.
media:content.media:description: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.

media:content.media:text: Horvath taught and worked as an educational consultant for the past decade, while writing on the side.
media:content.media:description: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.

item.title: The book fueling a movement against screens in schools

item.description: Protesters and police have been clashing outside of an immigration center in Newark, New Jersey, for more than a week. Newark’s mayor implemented a curfew for the area that will be in effect from 9 p.m. to 6 a.m. going forward after several people were arrested on Saturday. NBC’s Valerie Castro reports for Sunday TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Protests Outside Newark ICE Detention Center Enters 10th Day
media:content.media:description: Protesters and police have been clashing outside of an immigration center in Newark, New Jersey, for more than a week. Newark’s mayor implemented a curfew for the area that will be in effect from 9 p.m. to 6 a.m. going forward after several people were arrested on Saturday. NBC’s Valerie Castro reports for Sunday TODAY.

item.title: Protests Outside Newark ICE Detention Center Enters 10th Day

item.description: The NBA Finals are now set after the San Antonio Spurs won a dramatic Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals against reigning champions, the Oklahoma City Thunder. The Spurs, who are just a couple of years removed from being the among the worst ranked teams in the league, will host the New York Knicks in Game 1 of the finals.

media:content.media:title: Spurs Beat Reigning Champion Thunder to Face Knicks in Finals
media:content.media:description: The NBA Finals are now set after the San Antonio Spurs won a dramatic Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals against reigning champions, the Oklahoma City Thunder. The Spurs, who are just a couple of years removed from being the among the worst ranked teams in the league, will host the New York Knicks in Game 1 of the finals.

item.title: Spurs Beat Reigning Champion Thunder to Face Knicks in Finals

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
Dennis Romero
Mirna Alsharif
item.description: The driver of a bus in a crash that killed five people in Virginia on Friday, including a family of four en route to a relative’s wedding, has been charged with involuntary manslaughter

media:content.media:text: Bus after the crash.

item.title: Bus driver in Virginia crash that killed 5, including family, charged with manslaughter

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear, D-Ky., on Sunday said the Texas Senate race is “in play” for Democrats after state Attorney General Ken Paxton beat incumbent John Cornyn in last week’s Senate Republican primary.

media:content.media:text: Andy Beshear.
media:content.media:description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear said "Texas is in play" for Democrats after state Attorney General Ken Paxton defeated incumbent John Cornyn in the Senate GOP primary last week.

item.title: Andy Beshear says ‘Texas is in play’ after Ken Paxton wins Senate GOP primary

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: The wife of Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner said she is “really angry” and “disappointed” about reports that she had told her husband’s campaign last year that he had exchanged sexually explicit texts with other women.“It makes me really angry, disappointed, and I find it really shameful that there’s a group of media outlets and people who are willing to spread gossip instead of talking about real issues that Graham is is running on,” Platner’s wife, Amy Gertne

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner speaks
media:content.media:description: The wife of Graham Platner, the presumptive Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, disclosed to his campaign last year that he had exchanged sexually explicit texts with other women.

item.title: Graham Platner’s wife ‘angry’ her disclosures of his extramarital sexting were made public

item.dc:creator: Lauren Wilson
item.description: A24’s “Backrooms” — from 20-year-old Kane Parsons — broke box office records this weekend, marking the latest example of an indie horror hit helmed by a YouTuber.

media:content.media:text: A three way split composite image of Kane Parsons, left, Curry Barker, and Markiplier.
media:content.media:description: From left, Kane Parsons, Curry Barker, Markiplier.

media:content.media:text: Renate Reinsve stands in front of a wall with a rectangle drawn in blue lines, and her shadow is cast next to her on the wall
media:content.media:description: From left, Kane Parsons, Curry Barker, Markiplier.

item.title: Meet Hollywood’s new masters of horror: YouTubers

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Claude Lemieux’s brain is being donated to the Boston University CTE Center to research the long-term effects of repetitive brain injuries, his family said Saturday in a statement released by daughter Claudia Lemieux Bishop

media:content.media:text: Claude Lemieux.
media:content.media:description: Former Montreal Canadien's player Claude Lemieux in 1993.

item.title: Claude Lemieux’s brain is being donated to Boston University’s CTE Center, family says

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the second administration of President Donald Trump has “departed” from traditional conservative principle in the president’s second term.

media:content.media:text: Mike Pence at the Pray Vote Stand Summit in Washington, D.C.,
media:content.media:description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration has "departed" from core conservative principles.

item.title: Mike Pence says the Trump administration has ‘departed’ from conservative principles

item.dc:creator: Erika Edwards
item.description: The experimental drug daraxonrasib, which doubled survival time in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, may also prove effective for lung, colon and ovarian cancers.

media:content.media:text: Daraxonrasib is taken as three pills once a day. The experimental pill has been fast-tracked by the Food and Drug Administration for approval.
media:content.media:description: Daraxonrasib is taken as three pills once a day. The experimental pill has been fast-tracked by the Food and Drug Administration for approval.

media:content.media:text: Dr. Wolpin
media:content.media:description: Daraxonrasib is taken as three pills once a day. The experimental pill has been fast-tracked by the Food and Drug Administration for approval.

media:content.media:text: Debby and Ron Orcutt.
media:content.media:description: Daraxonrasib is taken as three pills once a day. The experimental pill has been fast-tracked by the Food and Drug Administration for approval.

item.title: After new drug’s ‘unprecedented’ results for pancreatic cancer, doctors look at other uses

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: Hundreds of diplomats have been forced to retire or were laid off in the Trump administration

media:content.media:text: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.
media:content.media:description: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.

media:content.media:text: Kelly Adams-Smith.
media:content.media:description: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.

media:content.media:text: U.S. Department of State.
media:content.media:description: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.

media:content.media:text: Health workers in gear.
media:content.media:description: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.

media:content.media:text: Rubio Briefing 5/5/26
media:content.media:description: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.

media:content.media:text: U.S. Special Envoy to the Middle East Steve Witkoff and President Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner in Islamabad on April 12, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Dozens of senior career foreign service officers have left the State Department since Trump took office.

item.title: U.S. faces foreign crises with a hollowed out diplomatic corps

item.dc:creator: Tyler Kingkade
item.description: “The Digital Delusion” by Jared Cooney Horvath connects the collapse in standardized test scores to the rise of education technology in public schools.

media:content.media:text: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.
media:content.media:description: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.

media:content.media:text: Jared Cooney Horvath predicted ed tech would be the next big debate after schools banned cellphones.

media:content.media:text: Parents brought copies of "The Digital Delusion" to give to school board members in Thousand Oaks, Calif., on Feb. 4.

media:content.media:text: Horvath taught and worked as an educational consultant for the past decade, while writing on the side.
media:content.media:description: Jared Cooney Horvath self-published "The Digital Delusion" in December 2025.

item.title: The book fueling a movement against screens in schools

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and captured a strategic mountain in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in 26 years.

media:content.media:text: An Israeli flag flies over the medieval Beaufort Castle, known locally as Qalaat al-Shaqif or Shaqif Arnoun, as seen from the Marjayoun area of southern Lebanon on May 31.
media:content.media:description: An Israeli flag flies over the medieval Beaufort Castle, known locally as Qalaat al-Shaqif or Shaqif Arnoun, as seen from the Marjayoun area of southern Lebanon on May 31.

item.title: Netanyahu vows to expand Israel’s grip on Lebanon after deepest incursion in 26 years

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: For the eighth consecutive year, the NBA will have a new champion

media:content.media:text: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs dribbles the ball during the game against the Oklahoma City Thunder during Game Seven of the NBA Western Conference Finals on May 30, 2026 at Paycom Center in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma.
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama of the San Antonio Spurs dribbles the ball during the game against the Oklahoma City Thunder during Game 7 of the NBA Western Conference finals on Saturday.

item.title: Spurs advance to NBA Finals, ousting reigning champion Thunder

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: The remains of all missing victims in a Washington state paper mill implosion have been recovered, officials announced Saturday.“Today, on day five of this incident, I can share that we have recovered the ninth and final missing employee of this incident,” Longview Fire Chief Brad Hannig said in a Saturday news conference

media:content.media:text: A chemical tank, seen from the air, leans to its side after collapsing inside an industrial factory yard.
media:content.media:description: The Nippon Dynawave Packaging plant in Longview, Wash., on Monday. Following the tank implosion, officials have been diluting the high-pH water in contaminated ditches, officials said.

media:content.media:text: A young woman places an electric candle down among dozens of others at a makeshift memorial.
media:content.media:description: The Nippon Dynawave Packaging plant in Longview, Wash., on Monday. Following the tank implosion, officials have been diluting the high-pH water in contaminated ditches, officials said.

item.title: All missing victims recovered in Washington paper mill explosion

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Jay Blackman
item.description: A meteor traveling at tens of thousands of miles an hour triggered a loud boom and ground shaking that were felt across Massachusetts on Saturday afternoon

media:content.media:text: An aerial view of downtown Boston.
media:content.media:description: An aerial general view of Boston on August 11, 2024.

item.title: Meteor triggers loud boom and shaking felt across Massachusetts

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain won back-to-back Champions League trophies by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Puskas Arena on Saturday

media:content.media:text: Paris Saint Germain celebrate.
media:content.media:description: Marquinhos of Paris Saint Germain lifts the trophy alongside teammates during the UEFA Champions League Final 2026 match between Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal FC at Puskas Arena on Saturday in Budapest, Hungary.

item.title: PSG wins back-to-back Champions League titles after shootout victory against Arsenal

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Coco Gauff finally met a player in Paris who could match her court coverage in long baseline rallies

media:content.media:text: Coco Gauff holds one hand over her face on the tennis court.
media:content.media:description: Coco Gauff of the United States during her third round match against Anastasia Potapova of Austria in Paris on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Naomi Osaka on a clay tennis court wearing a sequined outfit.
media:content.media:description: Coco Gauff of the United States during her third round match against Anastasia Potapova of Austria in Paris on Saturday.

item.title: Coco Gauff’s French Open title defense ends in 3rd-round loss

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday suggested canceling planned musical performances at the Great American State Fair in Washington this summer after several artists pulled out of the event.“We should have a giant MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN RALLY, for 250, instead of having overpriced singers, who nobody wants to hear, whose music is boring, and yet who do nothing but complain,” Trump said in a post on Truth Social Saturday afternoon.“Cancel it,” Trump said, before launching into a separate

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump speaking while half-obscured by a white column outside the White House.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump outside the White House on May 25, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Young MC, left, and Morris Day both declined to participate after saying they learned the event was political in nature.
media:content.media:description: Young MC, left, and Morris Day both declined to participate after saying they learned the event was political in nature.

item.title: Trump suggests canceling performances at ‘Great American State Fair’ after artists back out

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
Dennis Romero
item.description: Tensions rose at a Newark, New Jersey, immigration detention center on Saturday as a group of pro-ICE protesters faced off with demonstrators who have maintained a presence outside the facility for more than a week in support of detainees who they say are enduring inhumane conditions inside

media:content.media:text: Delaney Hall protest.
media:content.media:description: Newark police arrive outside the Delaney Hall detention center on May 30, 2026, in Newark, N.J.

media:content.media:text: Protest outside Delaney Hall detention center, in Newark.
media:content.media:description: Barriers separate anti-ICE protesters from pro-ICE demonstrators outside the Delaney Hall detention center, in Newark, N.J., on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: An anti-ICE protesters holds a sign opposing a line of New Jersey State Police.
media:content.media:description: An anti-ICE protesters holds a sign opposing a line of New Jersey State Police.

media:content.media:text: Delaney Hall protest.
media:content.media:description: New Jersey State Police arrive with barricades on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Delaney Hall protest.
media:content.media:description: Pro-ICE supporters outside Delaney Hall

item.title: Clashes outside New Jersey ICE facility prompt curfew
